records.ety: [OE. venquishen, venquissen, venkisen, F. vaincre, pret. vainquis, OF. veintre, pret. venqui, venquis (cf. an OF. infin. vainquir), fr. L. vincere; akin to AS. wīg war, battle, wīgend a warrior, wīgan to contend, fight, OHG. wīgant a warrior, wīgan to fight, Icel. vīg battle, Goth. weihan to fight, contend. Cf. Convince, Evict, Invincible, Victor.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vanquished (v"kwsht); p. pr. & vb. n. Vanquishing.]

defs: To conquer, overcome, or subdue in battle, as an enemy.
quotes: They . . . vanquished the rebels in all encounters.

defs: Hence, to defeat in any contest; to get the better of; to put down; to refute.
quotes: This bold assertion has been fully vanquished in a late reply to the Bishop of Meaux's treatise.
For e'en though vanquished, he could argue still.

defs: A disease in sheep, in which they pine away.
